proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 123 anonymized features in ds001_nsclc is associated with objective_response (overall ORR ~16.9%) at p<0.05 after a univariate screen using chi-square tests for categorical features and Welch t-tests for continuous features.
The three-level categorical feature_051 (levels 0/1/2 with counts 17557/24962/7481) is associated with objective_response, with ORR differing across the three levels.
Higher values of the continuous feature_011 (range 0.0-28.0) are associated with lower probability of objective_response.
The binary feature_013 is negatively associated with objective_response: ORR is lower in patients with feature_013=1 than in patients with feature_013=0.

analyses.code: for c in features: chi2_contingency or ttest_ind vs objective_response; rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen of 123 features. 13 features significant at p<0.05; 8 at p<0.001. Top hits (effect = ORR difference, signed): feature_051 multi-cat ORR 0.211/0.157/0.111 (chi-square p=2.9e-93); feature_011 mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (Welch t p=2.8e-35); feature_013 ORR 0.154 (=1) vs 0.196 (=0), p=1.1e-33; feature_067 ORR 0.144 vs 0.177, p=2.0e-18; feature_006 ORR 0.182 vs 0.164, p=1.0e-6; feature_099 mean 3.821 vs 3.798, p=1.3e-4; feature_063 mean 5.78 vs 6.15, p=4.2e-4; feature_007 ORR 0.175 vs 0.164, p=5.9e-4. h1.1, h1.2, h1.3, h1.4 all supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on objective_response is monotone-decreasing across its three ordered levels (0 -> 1 -> 2), consistent with an ordered clinical variable such as a PD-L1 / treatment-line / stage gradient.
The negative association between continuous feature_011 and objective_response is approximately monotone across deciles of feature_011 (highest decile lower ORR than lowest decile).

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_051 level: 0 -> 0.2108 (n=17557); 1 -> 0.1570 (n=24962); 2 -> 0.1105 (n=7481). Monotone decreasing across levels. Confirmed in logistic regression: vs reference 0, level 1 log-odds -0.367 (p<1e-15), level 2 log-odds -0.776 (p<1e-15); coefficients are nearly equally spaced, supporting an ordinal interpretation.
ORR by feature_011 deciles (lowest -> highest): 0.192, 0.182, 0.167, 0.172, 0.159, 0.157, 0.143, 0.132. Approximately monotone decreasing from 19.2% to 13.2% across the range. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a logistic regression including feature_051 (categorical), feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, race (feature_123), insurance (feature_005), smoking (feature_057), histology (feature_043), and age (feature_078), each of feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 retains a significant independent association with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, age (feature_078, years) shows no significant association with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, race (feature_123) and smoking status (feature_057) are not significantly associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051) + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + C(feature_123) + C(feature_005) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_043) + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds (p): feature_051[T.1] -0.367 (p<1e-15); feature_051[T.2] -0.776 (p<1e-15); feature_011 -0.039 per unit (p<1e-15); feature_013 -0.302 (p<1e-15); feature_067 -0.259 (p<1e-15); feature_006 +0.130 (p=1.8e-6); feature_007 +0.080 (p=8.6e-4); feature_039 +0.076 (p=1.6e-3). Age feature_078 +0.0004 per year (p=0.76, NS). Race (feature_123) all levels NS (smallest p=0.13 for white vs asian reference). Smoking (feature_057) all levels NS (p>=0.69). Histology squamous vs adeno +0.041 (p=0.14, NS). Insurance: private vs medicaid +0.089 (p=0.019). h3.1, h3.2, and h3.3 all supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is larger (more negative log-odds) at higher feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_013).
The negative effect of feature_067 on objective_response differs across feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_067).
The negative slope of feature_011 on objective_response differs across feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_011).

analyses.result_summary: feature_051 main effects unchanged. feature_013 main log-odds -0.282 (p<1e-13). Interaction terms: feature_051[T.1]:feature_013 = -0.061 (p=0.241); feature_051[T.2]:feature_013 = +0.104 (p=0.218). Neither interaction is significant; effect of feature_013 is essentially constant across feature_051 levels. h4.1 NOT supported.
feature_067 main log-odds -0.248 (p=2e-8). Interaction terms: feature_051[T.1]:feature_067 = -0.011 (p=0.857); feature_051[T.2]:feature_067 = -0.048 (p=0.635). Both NS. h4.2 NOT supported.
feature_011 main log-odds -0.034 per unit (p<1e-10). Interaction terms: feature_051[T.1]:feature_011 = -0.011 (p=0.113); feature_051[T.2]:feature_011 = +0.002 (p=0.887). Both NS. h4.3 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The distribution of feature_051 (a candidate treatment / PD-L1 / stage variable) differs across self-reported race (feature_123) categories - i.e., the cohort shows allocation disparities by race.
The distribution of feature_051 differs across insurance categories (feature_005), suggesting differential utilization by payer.
The effect of feature_051 on objective_response (responsiveness) differs by self-reported race (interaction feature_051 x feature_123).
The effect of feature_051 on objective_response differs by insurance category (interaction feature_051 x feature_005).

analyses.result_summary: Cross-tabulation of feature_051 by race shows nearly identical proportions at each level across asian/black/hispanic/other/white (level-2 share 0.145 / 0.146 / 0.153 / 0.136 / 0.150). Chi-square test p=0.639. h5.1 NOT supported - no allocation disparity by race.
feature_051 distribution by insurance: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured level-2 share 0.143/0.150/0.151/0.152. Chi-square p=0.248. h5.2 NOT supported - no allocation disparity by insurance.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051)*C(feature_123)', data=df).fit() vs main-effects model; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test for race x feature_051 interaction: chi2=1.964, df=8, p=0.982. ORR within each race subgroup follows the same monotone pattern across feature_051 levels (e.g., white 0.207/0.154/0.107; black 0.219/0.164/0.125). h5.3 NOT supported.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051)*C(feature_005)', data=df).fit() vs main-effects model; LR test
analyses.result_summary: Likelihood-ratio test for insurance x feature_051 interaction: chi2=7.74, df=6, p=0.258. h5.4 NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The five 0-4 ordinal features (feature_026, feature_033, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042) are each monotonically associated with objective_response (e.g., higher value -> lower ORR).
Smoking status (feature_057: never/former/current) is associated with objective_response, with never-smokers having different ORR than current/former smokers.
Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square p-values: feature_026 p=0.103; feature_033 p=0.207; feature_018 p=0.50; feature_045 p=0.195; feature_042 p>0.5. ORRs across the 5 levels of each are within ~1-2 percentage points of each other and not monotone. h6.1 NOT supported - none of these ordinal features show meaningful associations with ORR.
ORR by smoking: current 17.2%, former 16.8%, never 16.4%. Chi-square p=0.40 (univariate). After multivariable adjustment, all smoking coefficients NS (p>=0.69). h6.2 NOT supported.
ORR adenocarcinoma 16.7% vs squamous 17.4%. Univariate p=0.078; adjusted p=0.14. Borderline NS difference. h6.3 NOT supported at conventional alpha.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 36 continuous features, feature_011 has the strongest negative association with objective_response per standard deviation, and at least three additional continuous features (e.g., feature_099, feature_063, feature_092) show significant per-SD associations.
Higher feature_099 (range 1.7-5.5, plausibly serum albumin in g/dL) is associated with higher probability of objective_response.

Higher feature_063 (right-skewed, range 0.03-296.9, plausibly an inflammatory marker) is associated with lower probability of objective_response, and the association is better described on the log scale.

analyses.code: for each continuous c: smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ {c}'). Rank by per-SD log-odds.
analyses.result_summary: Per-SD log-odds (p): feature_011 -0.149 (p=1.8e-32); feature_099 +0.046 (p=1.4e-4); feature_063 -0.044 (p=6.9e-4); feature_092 +0.035 (p=3.2e-3). All other 32 continuous features have |per-SD log-odds| <= 0.021 with p>=0.08. h7.1 supported.

feature_099 univariate: log-odds +0.091 per unit (p=1.4e-4). After multivariable adjustment in the full model, feature_099 retains log-odds +0.092 per unit (p=1.2e-4). Direction positive as predicted. h7.2 supported.

analyses.code: AIC of logit(orr ~ feature_063) vs logit(orr ~ log1p(feature_063))
analyses.result_summary: Raw feature_063: log-odds -0.0049 per unit (p=6.9e-4), AIC 45412. log1p(feature_063): log-odds -0.071 per log-unit (p<<1e-4), AIC 45401 (10.8 lower; better fit). In full multivariable model log1p(feature_063) log-odds -0.074 (p=9e-7). Direction negative as predicted; log scale superior. h7.3 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative independent predictors of ORR) interact super-additively: the joint negative effect when both = 1 is more negative than the sum of the individual effects.
The slope of feature_011 on log-odds of ORR is steeper (more negative) in the feature_013=1 subgroup than in feature_013=0 (interaction).
The slope of feature_011 on log-odds of ORR differs by feature_067 status (interaction feature_011 x feature_067).

Among ~50/50-split binary features (potential demographic indicators), feature_039 is associated with objective_response while feature_086, feature_115, feature_037, feature_095, feature_093, feature_012, and feature_110 are not.

analyses.result_summary: ORR cells: f013=0,f067=0 -> 0.207 (n=13223); f013=0,f067=1 -> 0.165 (n=4412); f013=1,f067=0 -> 0.161 (n=24193); f013=1,f067=1 -> 0.132 (n=8172). Interaction log-odds = +0.039 (p=0.505). Joint effect is approximately additive on log-odds scale. h8.1 NOT supported.
feature_011:feature_013 interaction = -0.0041 per unit (p=0.538). NS. h8.2 NOT supported.
feature_011:feature_067 interaction = +0.0011 per unit (p=0.885). NS. h8.3 NOT supported.

analyses.code: for each ~50/50 binary feature: chi2_contingency vs y
analyses.result_summary: Among binary features with imbalance < 10%: feature_039 (n1=25008) ORR 0.174 vs 0.164, p=3.4e-3 (significant); feature_007 (n1=22371) ORR 0.175 vs 0.164, p=5.9e-4 (significant); feature_093 p=0.27, feature_086 p=0.72, feature_115 p=0.88, feature_037 p=0.91, feature_095 p=0.51, feature_053 p=0.067, feature_012 p=0.36, feature_110 p=0.68 (all NS). h8.4 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a consolidated multivariable logistic regression that includes feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, log1p(feature_063), feature_092, feature_076, feature_112, feature_021, race, insurance, smoking, histology, and age, all the strongest features (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067) remain significant at p<0.001 with their univariate signs preserved.
feature_076 is positively and feature_112 is negatively associated with objective_response after multivariable adjustment (both seen as borderline-significant in univariate screen).
feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.80, mean 0.31) is positively associated with objective_response after adjusting for the dominant predictor feature_051.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ C(feature_051) + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_099 + log_f063 + feature_092 + feature_076 + feature_112 + feature_021 + C(feature_123) + C(feature_005) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_043) + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full-model adjusted log-odds (p): feature_051[1] -0.368 (p<1e-15); feature_051[2] -0.778 (p<1e-15); feature_011 -0.039 (p<1e-15); feature_013 -0.302 (p<1e-15); feature_067 -0.259 (p<1e-15); feature_006 +0.130 (p=1.8e-6); feature_007 +0.080 (p=9.7e-4); feature_039 +0.077 (p=1.5e-3); feature_099 +0.093 (p=1.2e-4); log_f063 -0.074 (p=9e-7); feature_092 +0.176 per unit (p=2.0e-3); feature_076 +0.099 (p=0.021); feature_112 -0.102 (p=0.028); feature_021 -0.059 (p=0.064 NS); insurance private +0.088 (p=0.020), all other insurance NS; race all NS; smoking NS; histology NS; age NS. AIC 44535 vs nested smaller model AIC 44585. h9.1 supported (top 4 each p<<0.001 with original signs); h9.2 supported (feature_076 +, p=0.021; feature_112 -, p=0.028); h9.3 supported (feature_092 +0.176, p=0.002).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Predicted ORR for a typical white / medicare / former-smoker / adenocarcinoma / age-65 patient at mean lab values is between 9% and 22%, varying primarily as a function of feature_051 level (highest ORR at level 0, lowest at level 2).
Within feature_051 = 1, switching feature_013 from 1 to 0 (with all other variables at the typical-patient profile) raises predicted ORR by an absolute >=4 percentage points.
Predicted ORR is not materially different (absolute |delta| < 2 percentage points) when toggling self-reported race or insurance category in the typical-patient profile.

analyses.code: model.predict for typical-patient profiles at feature_051=0,1,2
analyses.result_summary: Predicted ORR at typical-patient profile (white, medicare, former smoker, adenocarcinoma, age 65, mean labs, feature_013=1, feature_067=0, feature_006=0, feature_007=1, feature_039=1): feature_051=0 -> 0.205; feature_051=1 -> 0.151 (baseline); feature_051=2 -> 0.106. Range across feature_051 ~ 10 percentage points absolute. h10.1 supported.

analyses.code: model.predict for profile feature_051=1, feature_013=1 vs feature_013=0
analyses.result_summary: Typical profile at feature_051=1, feature_013=1, feature_067=0 -> predicted ORR 0.151. Same with feature_013=0 -> predicted ORR 0.194. Absolute delta +4.3 percentage points (relative ~28% increase). h10.2 supported.

analyses.code: model.predict for typical profile across each value of feature_123 and feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds for race levels span -0.077 to +0.051 (absolute predicted-ORR delta < 1.5 percentage points across race categories at the typical profile). For insurance, predicted-ORR delta from medicaid -> private ~ +1.2 percentage points (only private vs medicaid is significant at p=0.02). All toggles within |delta| < 2 percentage points. h10.3 supported.
